violations.text: Beans observed held at 120 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Beans were reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024
Onions were observed stored on the floor near the hand washing sink in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen and on the front service line. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigerators have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024
The dumpster area has an accumulation of trash and leaf litter. The dumpster area must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024

The grassy area outside the hallway door to the dumpster has an accumulation of trash & litter. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024

The floor in the walk in cooler, and wall near the front line hand sink have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024
The sidewalk near the dumpster area has an accumulation of wood and pallets. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Chicken, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

Chlorine sanitizer solution in the dishwasher was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Service was called during inspection, and 3 bay sink will be used for sanitizing until dishwasher is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The door to the outside, located in the back hallway, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings on the floor of the kitchen, especially in the back area near the soda syrup rack, and beneath the dishwasher. Orkin PCS. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023
The dumpster area and sidewalk leading to it have an accumulation of food debris, trash, wood, and debris. Dumpster areas must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/24/2023
